title: Vulnlog
description: Vulnlog YAML format for tracking, analyzing, and managing software vulnerabilities reported by SCA scanners. Each file is scoped to a single release branch. See https://vulnlog.dev for the full specification.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version of the Vulnlog file.

tags.description: Tags for categorizing and scoping vulnerability entries. Provides flexible labeling for deliverables, usage contexts, or any team-specific taxonomy.

releases.description: Releases tracked in this Vulnlog file. Must be in chronological order (oldest first).

vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerability entries. May be empty. Newest entries first.

https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/proj.title: Vulnlog Project
https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/proj.description: Project-level metadata. Used in report generation and as author information in VEX documents.

organization.description: Name of the organization or vendor.

author.description: Name of the responsible security team or author. Used as the author in VEX documents.

contact.description: Contact email for the security team. Used as the author contact in VEX documents. Can be the contact from your security.txt.

https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/comm.title: Vulnlog Common Definitions
https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/comm.description: Shared types and formats used across the Vulnlog schema.

purl.description: Package URL (purl) as defined by the purl specification (https://github.com/package-url/purl-spec).

verdict.description: The human triage decision for a vulnerability. Absence indicates the vulnerability is under investigation.

justification.description: OpenVEX justification for why a vulnerability does not affect the project. Required when verdict is 'not_affected'.

reporterType.description: The scanner or source type. Determines the suppression file format for reporters with suppression support.

https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/rele.title: Vulnlog Release
https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/rele.description: A release tracked in this Vulnlog file. Releases must be listed in chronological order (oldest first).

published_at.description: Publication date of the release. Absence indicates the release is not yet published.

purls.description: Versioned Package URLs identifying the release artifacts. Used as product identifiers in VEX documents. Tags on purl entries enable scoped VEX generation.

purlEntry.description: A release artifact identified by its Package URL, optionally tagged for scoping.

purl.description: The versioned Package URL for this artifact in this release (e.g., 'pkg:docker/demo-project@8.1.1').

tags.description: Tags associated with this purl. Used to match vulnerability tags for scoped VEX generation.

https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/tag..title: Vulnlog Tag
https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/tag..description: A tag for categorizing and scoping vulnerability entries. Tags replace the previous deliverables and usage concepts, providing flexible labeling for any team-specific taxonomy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the tag. Referenced by vulnerability entries and release purl entries.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this tag represents.

https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/repo.title: Vulnlog Report Entry
https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/repo.description: Records that a specific SCA scanner or other source reported a vulnerability.

reporter.description: The scanner or source type that reported this vulnerability.

at.description: Date the vulnerability was reported by this scanner or source.

source.description: Description of the report source (e.g., 'Oracle Critical Patch Update April 2022'). Optional for scanner reporters. Required by the CLI for reporter type 'other'.

vuln_id.description: Scanner-specific vulnerability identifiers (e.g., 'SNYK-JAVA-LOG4J-2314720'). Used when generating scanner-specific suppression files.

suppressEntry.description: Controls time-limited suppression for a specific scanner.

expires_at.description: Expiration date of the suppression. After this date, the CLI no longer includes this entry in the generated suppression file.

https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/vuln.title: Vulnlog Vulnerability Entry
https://vulnlog.dev/schemas/v1/defs/vuln.description: A single vulnerability finding and the human verdict about it. The verdict reflects the most severe impact across all listed packages.

id.description: Primary identifier of the vulnerability. Preferably a CVE ID. May be a scanner-specific ID if no CVE exists.

name.description: Common name of the vulnerability, if one exists (e.g., 'Log4Shell', 'Heartbleed'). Displayed prominently in reports.

aliases.description: Alternative identifiers for the same vulnerability (e.g., GHSA IDs, scanner-specific IDs). Used for cross-referencing in reports.

releases.description: Release identifiers affected by this vulnerability. Must reference releases defined in the releases section.

description.description: Brief human-readable description of the vulnerability. Optional to allow quick stub entries during triage.

reports.description: Scanner reports that identified this vulnerability.

tags.description: Tags for categorizing and scoping this vulnerability. Must reference tags defined in the tags section. Used for filtering CLI output and scoping VEX products.

analysis.description: Free-text analysis and rationale for the triage decision.

analyzed_at.description: Date the analysis was performed. Must not be before the earliest report date.

comment.description: Additional information not related to the analysis itself. May include evidence references, methodology notes, decision-making context, or reminders for future review. Not used in any output generation.

fixed_in.description: Release in which the vulnerability was resolved. Required when verdict is 'fixed'.

fixed_at.description: Date the fix was applied. Only meaningful when verdict is 'fixed'.

fix_ref.description: Reference to the issue or ticket tracking the fix (e.g., a Jira issue URL).

fix_note.description: Brief description of how the vulnerability was fixed.
